

**Member Handbook**

*for the*

Saigon Equities Forum

*![][image1]*

# *Effective January 25, 2026* 

# **Foreword**

*Welcome to the Saigon Equities Forum\!*

We’re super, super glad to have you onboard. You’re joining a team of talented and motivated students from around the world, all hoping to gain exposure into Vietnamese finance and carve out their own space and voice. Our principal goal in SEF is to together learn and make mistakes, making professional-grade equity reports, investment theses and strategies within an educational context, giving each other a platform to hone analytical skills before you apply it in the real-world. 

**We prioritize teamwork, communication, initiative and accountability.** Everyone has to hold ourselves accountable to ensure that our behavior stays up to our brand. 

This is our first season that SEF is in existence, and we are bootstrapping everything together to form an organization that ensures it can be passed down to future generations. In a sense, what you’re doing will not only benefit you, but others in the future as you move through SEF with us and work together with new members as they inherit SEF’s ecosystem. Moreover, our deliverables are seen by real, potential recruiters and referral leads. Hence, we have to ensure our work is produced to the highest standards possible.

If at any time you need assistance from any of us, feel free to ask\! We operate on an open-door policy where you are welcome to book a meeting with us to discuss anything.

Nam Nguyen  
3th October, 2025

# 

# **Purpose**

This handbook outlines the expectations, tools, frameworks, and resources that’ll equip you for success during your time here. It is currently in effect for the 2025-2026 season.

This handbook is not a hard contract. It does not create a warranty, express or implied, guaranteeing you (referred hereby as the “member”) any specific staffing term, nor does it obligate you to maintain your position for a specific duration. However, do understand that failure to adhere can affect the work of the organization (also referred as the “community”); refractions may result in administrative consequences determined by the Board of Directors. 

The directors (referred hereby as “we”) reserves the right to modify any policies and procedures as needed. We will make every effort to notify you of such changes via email and other relevant channels. However, a formal notice isn't a prerequisite for these changes to come into effect.

# **SEF Introduction**

**Saigon Equities Forum (SEF)** is a student-led financial research platform with a focus on Vietnam. We produce comprehensive research reports and insights on Vietnam’s macroeconomy and capital markets, including listed companies and industries on the VN-Index.

Consisting of two Macro and Equity Research teams, SEF brings together students from renowned institutions spanning across Asia, the US, Europe, and Australia, under one common purpose to study, analyze, and promote Vietnam’s financial and geopolitical landscape.

**Goal**: To create a platform where ideas on Vietnam’s capital markets can be debated, research is accessible and shared freely, and the next generation of thinkers can engage in reshaping their community.

# 

# **Values**

**Integrity**  
We strive to be honest, unbiased, and transparent with our research ethics, upholding the highest standards of academic excellence. Our work remains independent and free from vested interests.

**Collaboration**  
We strive to bring together voices across multiple disciplines, fostering constructive dialogue and ensuring well-informed and well-rounded perspectives in our work.

**Public Service**  
We work with the community in mind — promoting financial literacy, sharing knowledge openly, and ensuring that our research serves the broader public good.

# 

# **Getting Started**

**By now, your director will have conducted an onboarding meeting with you to go over SEF’s mission, expectations, and next steps. If not, please contact them to schedule one.**

SEF has two analyst divisions: Economics & Policy; Equity Research.

### **Economics & Policy**

**Job Description**: Conduct analysis in and report on macro trends, sector insights, regional comparisons

**Tools**: CafeF, FactSet and LSEG Workspace Terminal (if able), Excel, Figma, Canva

**Deliverables:**

- “Vietnam Macro Tracker” → monthly post with GDP, inflation, FX (clean chart \+ 3 insights).  
- “Policy Watch” → one-pager when SBV changes rates or new laws come out.  
- “Regional Comparisons” → simple ASEAN charts showing Vietnam vs peers.

### **Equity Research**

**Job Description**: Conduct analysis in and report on HOSE and HNX equities

**Tools**: CafeF, FactSet and LSEG Workspace Terminal (if able), Excel, Figma, Canva

**Deliverables:**

- Short “Company Snapshots” → pick 1 company per month, 2–3 charts \+ commentary.  
- “Sector Mini-Notes” → banks, consumer goods, energy; tables with ratios.  
- “Valuation Spotlight” → 1 multiple (e.g., EV/EBITDA) explained in a post with a live company example.

*We are yet to have a design team or outreach team. Members of the analyst divisions should be able to do graphic design by themselves and promote their own material, as it is your work that you take credit for.*

### 

### **Analyst Coverage**

# **Policies and Practices**

**Communication**  
We mainly utilize English as our working language, as SEF members come from different backgrounds, nationalities, and life experiences. Please uphold an environment of respect for others. While it is okay to crack jokes here and there, refrain from directly or indirectly hurting someone.

**Confidentiality**  
We take confidentiality very seriously, please report violations of SEF material being leaked to directors and co-founders.

**Time commitment**  
\~6-8 hours / week

**Promotions**  
Promotions to Senior Analyst roles are decided on a consensus basis in the Board of Directors. While there is no fixed framework to decide promotions, decisions are made through factoring in the member’s commitment, quality of their work, proactiveness in assisting others, and overall contributions to SEF.

**Quality Assurance**  
After a project has been completed, it is still considered a draft if not yet approved by directors or through Weekend Roundtable. 

**Overview of QA System:**

- Initial assignment  
- After first draft is done, project goes through WR or directors for cross-check, providing constructive feedback  
- After the project is amended, it is resubmitted through WR or directors for a final check before it is launched.

*For analysts: Please ensure your data and sources (news articles, research reports, data portals, Excel files)are traceable to its source and auditable. We use APA as a citation guide.*

**Open-door policy**  
Our co-founders have an open-door policy. In any case should you require assistance, feel free to contact us via [our directory](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1Kyvgy83w_J2ocPin94Rm7FE2Lj7wl4zndkBcuZb9oGQ/edit?gid=0#gid=0) and book a meeting.

# **Meeting and Workshop Framework**

Meetings will be announced beforehand after consensus on meeting time is reached via When2Meet.

**Weekend Roundtable (WR)**  
Every Saturday weekend, all members will come together to present work they have done so far and what to expect for the next week. We’ll also go over where we are on the SEF timeline and see if we are on time.

**Workshops**  
Occasionally, directors will host a workshop on aspects such as financial account fundamentals (e.g. Valuation, DCF modeling, finding reports) and tools tutorials (LSEG, matplotlib, Figma) for members.

**Working Hour**  
We often organize working hours to bring members together in a common time to address outstanding issues that arise during production of a work, where constant communication between one or more members is necessary. To request a working hour, simply text our main group chat.

# **Deliverables**

**Equity Report (Investment Thesis)**: Usually taking 2-3 months, these detailed reports on Vietnamese equities are our main product. Whether it be on UpCOM, HNX or HOSE, we conduct academic research using online sources, then perform valuations to issue academic recommendations. Equity reports are the work of the entire team, both the Equity and Macro department combined. **It is (and you should too) emphasized that since these are purely academic material intended to hone macro and valuation skills, they are not investment advice. Doing so would be illegal.**

**Macro Outlook:** Another important product is macro/industry reports on the Vietnamese business scene. Usually taking 1 month, these detailed reports are a culmination of combining external macro research with our in-house insights using knowledge gained from traditional economics training in school. This is mainly the responsibility of the Macro department.

**Industry and Macro Snapshots:** Usually taking one week, these reports are similar to above, yet these are intended to be on the short-form, digestible side for our social media pages. Both the Macro and Equity department can partake in creating a snapshot.

# **Resources**

**Figma**: We make our social media posts using Figma. The link is [here](https://www.figma.com/design/wgeoK7cwnQFqZwZxrIMjY0/Saigon-Equities-Forum?node-id=21-2&t=mlCxv7qMY04adNck-0), please request permission from either Nam or Hung.

**Matplotlib; R:** We prioritize Excel for our graphs, but in the case that graphs require the use of Matplotlib, please contact Jay Chang on Instagram or at [jayc29@berkeley.edu](mailto:jayc29@berkeley.edu) for further instructions on how to use it.

**LSEG Workspace; FactSet**: Please consult your school’s library for access, particularly in their economics or financial research section.

In cases where your school does not offer access to terminals, please contact your directors.

**Lark:** We also utilize Lark as a working platform for product management, to keep track of assignments, deadlines, priorities and strategies. Please contact Tan Ngo on Instagram or at [markie.ngo@marquette.edu](mailto:markie.ngo@marquette.edu) for further instructions on how to use it.

**External Sources File:** SEF has an in-house library that contains industry-standard material on finance and consulting training for your reference in producing deliverables for SEF, as well as for your own personal development as a finance professional. Resources include Breaking Into Wall Street guides, consulting casebooks from Ivy Leagues, Excel and CapitalIQ cheatsheets, investment banking interview tips from clubs of renowned universities. We also have books including Rosenbaum & Pearl’s Investment Banking, Erik Banks’ Finance The Basics.

The file is updated on an occasional basis. **Because many of these are paid and are internal, please do not bring any of the aforementioned materials outside SEF. Doing so constitutes a breach of our “Policies and Practices” section.**

# **Exiting SEF**

We appreciate your time here and we respect your decision; we will try our best to accommodate you as you move on to future endeavors.

In the case that you resign before the season ends, please inform your directors beforehand, preferably two weeks in advance, via email, text, or verbally.

During this time, we may ask for an exit interview for the purposes of feedback and reflection on our professional relationship, as well as improving SEF’s policies and practices.
